metadata.title: Why the 90-Day Clock Is a Gate, Not a Tiebreaker

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Aloha, Liam — in for Bear. Why? Can you explain it, Bear?

beats.name: Title
beats.narration_text: A student runs a perfect job search. Lands an offer at a sponsoring company. And can't take it.

beats.name: MeiOpenScene
beats.narration_text: Three months of OPT left. A role passes every filter. Proven sponsor. Live posting. Strong fit. Great quality. She applies.

props.headline: Three months of OPT left. A role passes every filter. Proven…

beats.scene_description: Young woman at a laptop reviewing job postings, editorial newsprint collage aesthetic, desaturated tones
beats.image_prompt: B02, young woman in her late 20s sitting at a desk with a laptop open to a job board, focused expression, soft editorial light from a window, newsprint collage aesthetic, desaturated tones, flat print reproduction, cream and charcoal palette

beats.name: TheCliff
beats.narration_text: Four months later, an offer arrives. The start date is past her authorization end. The offer is worthless.

beats.name: TheQuestion
beats.narration_text: A strong role should close before authorization ends. Here is the case where a perfect role became unreachable before the first interview. Why?

beats.name: TheScoringProblem
beats.narration_text: Every filter said yes. Sponsorship: real. Posting: live. Fit: high. Quality: high. The composite score looked excellent.

beats.name: TheHiddenConstraint
beats.narration_text: None of those filters know your dates. Sponsorship is visible to a scorer. Your authorization end date is not.

beats.name: ProcessTimeline
beats.narration_text: A hiring process has stages: apply, phone screen, technical rounds, panel, offer. Each stage takes time. The total can run months.

beats.name: TheGateLine
beats.narration_text: Draw a vertical line at authorization end. If the expected offer date lands past that line, the role is unreachable. The calendar killed it before the first email.

beats.name: MultiplierNotAddend
beats.narration_text: The timeline factor doesn't vote alongside sponsorship. It multiplies. A factor of zero zeroes the composite. Sponsorship score times zero equals zero.

beats.name: TheCostOfWaiting
beats.narration_text: Worse: the four months spent chasing this role consumed the buffer she needed for roles she could have landed. A zero-factor role doesn't just waste effort. It steals it.

beats.name: ThreeRoles
beats.narration_text: Mei has four months of OPT left. Role A: MegaCorp, five-month process. Expected start past authorization. Factor zero. Skip — before excitement sets in. Role B: startup, six-week process. Factor near one. Other factors decide. Both look identical until you draw the line. Illustrative numbers.

beats.name: Endcard
beats.narration_text: Process length is invisible to every scoring factor except the timeline gate. The gate doesn't vote. It multiplies. A factor of zero cancels everything else.

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Here's what the body just demonstrated. Worse: the four months spent chasing this role consumed the buffer she needed for roles she could have landed. A zero-factor role doesn't just waste effort. It steals it. Mei has four months of OPT left. Role A: MegaCorp, five-month process. Expected start past authorization. Factor zero. Skip — before excitement sets in. Role B: startup, six-week process. Factor near one. Other factors decide. Both look identical until you draw the line. Illustrative numbers.

props.artifactHeading: Why the 90-Day Clock Is a Gate, Not
artifactLines: A hiring process has stages: apply, phone screen, technical rounds, panel, offer.
Draw a vertical line at authorization end.
Worse: the four months spent chasing this role consumed the buffer she needed for roles…
Process length is invisible to every scoring factor except the timeline gate.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Why the 90-Day Clock Is a Gate, Not a Tiebreaker] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?

props.title: Why the 90-Day Clock Is a Gate, Not a Tiebreaker
props.subline: a factor of zero cancels everything else
